title: Domestic Violence Hotlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > Domestic Violence Hotlines 43 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Deafness Hispanic/Latino Community LGBTQ2+ Issues Native American Community CALL TO SAFETY DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral, advocacy and safety planning, and transportation from danger to safety. Transportation is limited and dependent on funding. Provides referrals for a Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA), which is required before accessing most direct domestic violence services. Also serves as an entry point into the Multnomah County Domestic Violence System Coordinated Access Program. PO Box 42610 Portland, OR 97242 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, sex trafficking, and/or sex industry work, and their supportive family and friends. Domestic and/or sexual violence must have occurred within the last six months. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Support line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 235-5333 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, confidential text and chat line, emergency shelter and transitional housing services, legal advocacy to assist with temporary restraining orders, safety planning, housing advocacy and financial empowerment programs, violence prevention, and community education. 256 Warner Milne Road Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault or sex industry, and older adults experiencing elder abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other assistance: Varies by service Email (888) 654-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and referral services about domestic violence, sexual assault, sex trafficking, homeless/runaway youth, and stalking. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE RESOURCE CENTER INC MONIKA'S HOUSE SHELTER MONIKA'S HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe confidential emergency shelter and a domestic violence crisis hotline for survivors fleeing intimate partner domestic violence. Services for shelter residents include advocacy, case management, and support groups. Eligibility: Domestic violence survivors of all ages and genders who are in imminent physical danger. On-site indoor and outdoor kennels for pets are available. Service and therapy animals are allowed in the shelter. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 469-8620 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, protective order assistance, emergency shelter, safety planning, court and medical accompaniment, referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 726 N 1st Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence, human trafficking, and elder abuse. Also serves individulas with disabilities who are victims of abuse. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 947-2449 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, safety planning, short-term shelter, counseling, court advocacy, support groups, and referral to and advocacy with local agencies and other programs that offer long-term shelter. For survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault, provides assistance with temporary restraining and stalking orders. Does not provide intervention services for perpetrators of violence. 420 E 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -Hours vary for other services Email (541) 296-1662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and a 30-day emergency shelter with six rooms for survivors of sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking. Offers medical and legal advocacy, including accompaniment to appointments and assistance with paperwork. Food, clothing, and peer counseling are available as well. PO Box 441 Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Anyone fleeing sexual assault, domestic violence, or stalking Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6603 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential crisis and support line for safety planning and referrals, a safe house, support groups, and community education to prevent intimate partner violence. Can assist with restraining orders and accompanying individuals to the Lane County Courthouse. Rural outreach provided by arrangement. 1577 Pearl Street Floor 2, Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence of any kind, regardless of gender, as well as their families and friends Hours: Crisis and Support Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Web Chat: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 485-6513 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline, support groups, and a variety of supportive services for survivors of intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and trafficking. Provides emergency shelter services, based on availability, for survivors. Also provides in-person response to the emergency room for sexual assault survivors and assistance with protective and stalking orders. Every person who contacts Klamath Advocacy Center gets five essential services for safety and independence: safety plan, risk assessment, needs assessment, resource information, and next steps. PO Box 1358 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Shelter: Women and women with minor children who are survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence or sexual assault All other services: Survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and/or stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 884-0390 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MAYDAY INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential hotline for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and elder abuse. Services include emergency shelter, assistance with filing restraining orders, transportation to healthcare facilities and court, advocacy, and legal support. Additional services include information and referrals, community education, training programs, and court advocacy. 1705 Main Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Crisis line hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 523-4134 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, emergency shelter, support groups, and legal advocacy (assistance with protective and stalking orders). 1202 SE Douglas Avenue Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing domestic abuse, sexual assault, human trafficking, or stalking. Shelter allows pets. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 673-7867 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT DOVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line for domestic violence, sexual assault, and human trafficking. Also provides shelter and advocacy. Eligibility: Survivors or those fleeing domestic violence, sexual assault, human trafficking, or child abuse Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (800) 889-2000 24 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SABLE HOUSE 24 HOUR HOTLINE AND CONFIDENTIAL SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and a confidential shelter for those in danger or attempting to escape safely from domestic violence. Offers in-person response for victims of sexual assault. 314 SE Oak Street Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Domestic or sexual violence survivors and their children Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 623-6703 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE HARBORS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault resources including a hotline, advocacy, classes, and shelter. 616 W North Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -Hours vary for other services Email (541) 263-1094 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline, information and referral, and counseling. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Any survivor of domestic violence or sexual assault Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day/ 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 397-6161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for domestic violence and sexual assault survivors. Also provides a crisis line, outreach services, support groups, and community education. 1681 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Coos Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Curry Outreach Office Tuesday - Friday 8am to 4pm Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 756-7000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. 990 NW Brooks Street Suite 1 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 389-7021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 475-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE PRINEVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 416-2114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE REDMOND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 504-2550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides crisis line for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and/or stalking. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Helpline and Crisis Advocacy: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIDES OF CHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual assault helpline and resource drop-in center. Services include counseling, support groups, advocacy, transportation, health advocacy, rental assistance, accompaniment services, protection order assistance, 24-hour crisis intervention, limited financial assistance, and a hotline. 1902 2nd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals who are the victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, trafficking, or dating violence Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-4pm, Wednesday noon-4pm Email (503) 842-9486 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WOMEN'S CRISIS SUPPORT TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, an emergency safe shelter for up to 13, and advocacy for survivors of domestic violence, stalking, and sexual assault. Offers prevention education in area high schools and throughout the community. The shelter has both individual and shared rooms. There are separate areas for men and women, with or without children. 612 NW 5th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Crisis line and advocacy: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking Shelter: Women or men, women or men and their children Hours: Crisis line and in-person response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, holiday hours may vary Email (541) 479-9349 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE CRISIS AND SUPPORT LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 24-hour crisis line that includes information and referrals to community resources, one-on-one peer support, and safety planning. 2208 SW 3rd Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Live Chat: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Email (541) 754-0110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line that includes safety planning. 605 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual abuse, human trafficking, and/or stalking, regardless of gender identity or sexual orientation Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 399-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO DE AYUDA DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential hotline for domestic violence and sexual assault survivors. Provides assistance in safety planning. 410 SW 9th Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 819-2544 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE CIRCLES OF HEALING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, information and referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and human trafficking. Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 808-8152 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION FAMILY VIOLENCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, rape, stalking, teen dating violence, and elder abuse with safety planning and guidance through the justice systems (Tribal, state, and federal). 46411 Timine Way Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic or intimate partner violence, sexual assault, rape, stalking, teen dating violence, and elder abuse Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm Email (541) 240-4171 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC PENDLETON ADVOCACY CENTER 24 HOUR CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line for domestic violence, dating violence, stalking, and sexual assault. Services include safety planning, confidential abuse assessments, and advocacy. 1103 SE Court Place Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 833-1161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON SLAVIC OREGON SOCIAL SERVICES SLAVIC OREGON SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific services including a domestic violence crisis support line, a sexual violence support line, intervention services, long-term case management, accompaniment to hospitals for forensic medical exams, assistance obtaining protective orders and communicating with law enforcement, and assistance in applying for crime victims' compensation and public benefits. May also include mental health counseling, assistance accessing low-cost/free immigration and other legal services, assistance with temporary/transitional housing, short-term financial assistance, support groups, economic empowerment, and short-term financial assistance. Eligibility: Individuals who are Slavic immigrants or refugees and actively experiencing or survivors of domestic violence or sexual violence Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 777-3437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line for domestic violence and sexual assault. Also provides case management, survivor advocacy, housing help, support groups, parenting support, community education, and help with restraining and stalking orders. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Hispanic and Latino domestic violence and sexual assault survivors Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours a day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 232-4448 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF GRANT COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, short-term emergency shelter, confidential walk-in center, peer counseling, transportation, temporary safe housing and access to transitional housing, support groups, medical and legal advocacy, outreach and education regarding domestic violence and sexual assault. 115 NW Bridge Street John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault Hours: Crisis line and shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm and by appointment Email (541) 620-1342 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a range of advocacy and support to survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault. Services include crisis intervention, court and medical accompaniment, peer advocacy, support groups, assistance with locating financial help, assistance with restraining and stalking orders, community education, referrals to meet emergency needs, and emergency shelter. 103 S Kerby Avenue Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed federal holidays Email (541) 592-2515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL DOMESTIC VIOLENCE HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential and anonymous crisis intervention, education, safety planning, and local referrals to anyone affected by intimate partner violence. Advocates have access to a database of local service providers and national resources throughout country. Offers dedicated hotlines for deaf, deafblind, or hard of hearing individuals. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 799-7233 1-800-799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic and sexual violence services, including safety planning, crisis intervention, advocacy, and educational services. Works with local programs to facilitate safety planning, provide transportation as necessary, and ensure the delivery of culturally relevant services in local shelters. Weekly domestic violence support groups that are closed groups held off-site for confidentiality reasons. Free child care is available. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence, primarily serves those who self-identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm. Support group times vary. Email (503) 288-8177 339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW BEGINNINGS INTERVENTION CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault including a hotline, counseling, legal advocacy, and support groups. 87127 Christmas Valley Highway Christmas Valley, OR 97641 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (541) 576-3051 24 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RAPHAEL HOUSE OF PORTLAND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter, a 24 hour crisis line, and community education focusing on safe and equitable relationships. Additional services for current and past Raphael House participants may include advocacy, youth programming, follow-up services, transitional housing, information and referral, support groups, counseling, and education workshops. Eligibility: Individuals and families who are survivors of domestic and intimate partner violence Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 222-6507 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a crisis line and emergency shelter. Advocacy includes information and referral, assistance with restraining orders, reporting options, counseling and support groups, and other supportive services. 10901 Island Avenue Island City, OR 97850 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence or sexual assault and their families and supporters Hours: Crisis Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 963-7226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource, referral, and crisis line that primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence, and human trafficking and stalking. In addition to providing a crisis line, offers support in the form of a domestic violence safe house, protection orders, advocacy, and support groups. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence; other emergency calls are accepted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 997-4444 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details STRONGHEARTS NATIVE HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers confidential and anonymous domestic and sexual violence helpline. Provides culturally-appropriate, peer-to-peer support as well as crisis intervention, assistance with safety planning, education, and referrals to Native-centered domestic and sexual violence service providers. PO Box 22293 Eagan, MN 55122 Eligibility: American Indian and Alaska Natives affected by domestic, dating, and sexual violence. Also serves affected family members, friends, service providers, and people who may question their abusive behavior. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (844) 762-8483 - 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE SALVATION ARMY WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER WEST WOMEN'S AND CHILDREN’S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to those escaping domestic violence. Crisis intervention services include emergency shelter, crisis line, advocacy, therapeutic children's programs, and support groups. Self-sufficiency programming includes transitional housing and advocacy, information and referral, and life skills training. Provides community education and educational materials. The program is 60 days with the potential of an extension to 90 days. Eligibility: Single women and women with children (male children must be under age 18) fleeing domestic violence, sexual assault, trafficking, intimate partner violence, or elder abuse Hours: Shelter hours and crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Client screenings: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 731-3900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CALL TO SAFETY DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES COMMUNITY WORKS HELPLINE DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HELPING HANDS AGAINST VIOLENCE HOTLINE AND EMERGENCY SERVICES HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER MAYDAY INC MAYDAY INC PEACE AT HOME ADVOCACY CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SHELTER AND SUPPORT SERVICES SABLE HOUSE 24 HOUR HOTLINE AND CONFIDENTIAL SHELTER SAFE HARBORS DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY SAFE PROJECT THE SAFE PROJECT SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE THE HARBOR HOTLINE TIDES OF CHANGE TIDES OF CHANGE WOMEN'S CRISIS SUPPORT TEAM WOMEN'S CRISIS SUPPORT TEAM CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE CRISIS AND SUPPORT LINE CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE CENTRO DE AYUDA DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS CIRCLES OF HEALING CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE UMATILLA INDIAN RESERVATION FAMILY VIOLENCE SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC 24 HOUR CRISIS LINE EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO UNICA HEART OF GRANT COUNTY HEART OF GRANT COUNTY ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER HEALING CIRCLE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE PROGRAM NEW BEGINNINGS INTERVENTION CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES SHELTER FROM THE STORM DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT
